# CrewAI Field Guide

> A concept-level field guide to the CrewAI codebase, focused on execution semantics —
> what actually happens when a crew or flow runs. Written by Doc Holiday
> (https://doc.holiday) by exploring the CrewAI source directly. Each page explains why a
> subsystem exists, how it behaves at runtime, and how it relates to its neighbors.
> Every doc page is also served as plain markdown at <page-url>/index.md.

## Pages

- [The Big Picture](/docs/the-big-picture/): the map of Crew, Agent, Task, Process, and Flow
- [Anatomy of a Kickoff](/docs/anatomy-of-a-kickoff/): one crew.kickoff() traced end to end
- [The Agent Executor Loop](/docs/the-agent-executor-loop/): inside a single agent turn — ReAct vs native tool calling
- [Context, Guardrails, and Retries](/docs/context-guardrails-and-retries/): how task outputs become the next task's context, and where guardrails sit
- [The Hierarchical Process](/docs/the-hierarchical-process/): what the manager agent really does — delegation as tools
- [Threads, asyncio, and the async barrier](/docs/threads-asyncio-and-the-async-barrier/): the concurrency model and its sharp edges
- [The Flow Scheduler](/docs/the-flow-scheduler/): how @start/@listen/@router methods are really scheduled
- [Where State Lives](/docs/where-state-lives/): memory, flow persistence, checkpoints, and replay
- [The LLM Layer](/docs/the-llm-layer/): the LLM factory — native provider SDKs vs the LiteLLM fallback
- [About This Site](/docs/about-this-site/): scope, audience, provenance

## See also

- Official CrewAI documentation: https://docs.crewai.com/
- CrewAI source: https://github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI
